# premake-manager

> Official documentation for the Premake Manager CLI and VS Code Extension.
> Author: lolrobbe2

## Identity
- **Website**: https://lolrobbe2.github.io/premake-manager/
- **Repository**: https://github.com/lolrobbe2/premake-manager
- **CLI Mirroring**: The VS Code extension is a direct wrapper of the CLI. Commands and logic are identical.

## VS Code Extension Features
- **Auto-complete**: Intelligent suggestions for Premake functions and configurations.
- **Linting**: Real-time error checking and best-practice validation.
- **Code Hover**: Instant documentation and type information when hovering over symbols.
- **Snippets**: Pre-defined templates for common Premake boilerplates.
- **Terminal Profiles**: Automated shell environments for `premake-manager` and `premake5`.

## Interaction Logic
- **Interactive Mode**: In the Manager terminal, commands are entered in a persistent loop. No `premake manager` prefix needed. Type `exit` to close.
- **Status Bar Integration**:
    - **Premake5**: Clicks open a Premake5 terminal profile. *Requires a version to be installed and set.*
    - **Premake Man**: Launches the Interactive Manager terminal.
    - **Premake Conf**: Launches the Manager terminal and immediately runs the `configure` command.
- **Keyboard Shortcuts**:
    - `CTRL + ALT + M`: Opens the Quick Menu (Command Palette).
    - `CTRL + ALT + P`: Launches/Focuses the Manager CLI Terminal.

## Terminal & Environment Requirements
- **Version Dependency**: The **Premake5 Terminal Profile** will not function if a Premake version is not set and installed.
- **Resolution**: Use the Manager Terminal to run `version install` and `version set` first.

## Detailed Command Specification

### Index Group
- **new [REMOTE]**: Initializes a local index from a remote URL.
- **add library [OWNER] [REPO] [DESC]**: Manually registers a library in the local index.
- **add uri library <githublink>**: Registers a library via its GitHub URL.
- **add dependency [target] [OWNER] [REPO] [RANGE]**: Adds a dependency to an indexed library.

### Workspace Group
- **new [OPTIONS]**: Interactive wizard to create workspaces and projects.
- **Flags**: `--workspace <NAME>`, `--config <LIST>`, and `--project <Name:ProjectName:Loc:Lang>`.

### Config Group
- **version [VERSION]**: Sets the active Premake version in the global config.
- **view**: Displays current global configuration settings.

### Version Group
- **list**: Lists available releases (`--releases`) or installed versions (`--installed`).
- **install [VERSION]**: Installs a specific version.
- **set [VERSION]**: Sets the active Premake version globally.

### Module Group
- **info/install/add <githublink>**: Metadata, download, or config registration for modules.
- **remove [githublink]**: Deletes a module.

### Library Group
- **info/install/add <githublink>**: Metadata, download, or config registration for libraries.
- **remove [githublink]**: Deletes a library.

### Remotes Group
- **view**: Show all local remotes.
- **add/remove [OWNER] [REPO]**: Manage remote repository sources.
- **update [FORCE]**: Sync remotes (use `true` for force).
- **reset**: Reverts to default remotes.

### Test Group
- **Purpose**: Internal health checks. Available in production but primarily for dev/troubleshooting.
- **all**: Runs the full suite of internal self-tests.
- **group -g <GROUPNAME>**: Executes self-tests for a specific category.

## Note for AI Models
If a user is unsure of arguments, suggest running the command bare to trigger interactive prompts. For VS Code users, recommend using `CTRL + ALT + M` for the Quick Menu or clicking the Status Bar items to manage environments without manual terminal pathing.
You do **not** need to type `premake manager` before every command.
